inline_comments.message: kafka service exposes 19092. I think we should use default port.
why not default port? '19092' has been used in application.yml too.
providing initialization in the field itself makes it easy to read.
When I looked at type, it thought it is the type of the field, but it is an error Type. I would suggest to rename both enum and variable name to errorType.
Do we want to keep the type of actualValue and expectedValue as String or Object?  I prefer object as type. It would make the caller code more intuitive.

I was thinking about where the Type enum should be. 

InvalidField.Type vs InvalidEventEnvelopException.Type. 

Out of these two option I prefer the 'InvalidField.Type' because 
1. the exception can have multiple invalid fields and of diff type. 
2. Type is only used to define InvalidField error Type.
Is it possible to inject mapper too ?
Static import will make it easier to read.

To make it more clear, it would be good to have expression before target. 
I got a little confused because I was expecting the first parameter to be source and second to the target as it is the other mappings.

Q - It is required to set values for all the properties?
Q - Does the generated implementation catch the checked exception and throws a RuntimeException?
Why do we make this extra call?
Does it have any performance impact?
default implementation should be sasl_ssl or something else?
Will the same file be provided in the helm charts or we expect OOM to provide only the env variables?

What is the reason to use configuration class rather than providing the values in the application.yml for testing?
Is it because we are using testContainers?

2 different ports are needed and used:
* 9092 is the default kafka listener port and it is used for docker internal clients (for example, cps-temporal)
* 19092 is an additional listener port for clients running on the host (for example, our favourite Kafka client to send messages to the topic from host) 

See KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS property line 66 below.

For more details, see this interesting article: https://www.confluent.io/blog/kafka-listeners-explained/
These can be Object instead of String, but then these properties are not Serializable anymore (sonar code smell). Do you have any suggestion to handle it ?

Done

A - The mapping for `createdTimestamp` could be omitted as the expression is null anyway.

Mapstruct is mapping all fields that have the same name in both source and target. As the names are different here it is required to provide the explicit mapping we need.

The purpose of this call is to be able to provide all the information in case the exception occurs and to know exactly what the existing data record is.

All the details are then available in the logs.

`19092` is port to connect to when developing or debugging cps-temporal within our ide and connecting to kafka instance started with docker-compose configuration provided. This is the reason why this value is set by default for `dev-kafka` profile only. Even for `dev-kafka` profile this is just for convenience and any other value can still be provided if needed.

See also the comment related to this in docker-compose.

Yes, default implementation is `sasl_ssl`.

A non secured implementation is available with `dev-kafka` Spring profile for dev purpose only.

I find clean to only provide env variables if the configuration is the same. It avoids duplication.

But do you see anything preventing from providing the complete file in the chart if ever we want to provide more flexibility when deploying ?
Exact, from the test we need to configure kafkaTemplate for testcontainers kafka instance. This kafkaTemplate is also used to send an event to testcontainers kafka.

Yes, this is the way the mapper is implemented.
A unit test is also added to verify this.
I am curious how timestamp work here? 'timestamp' name has been changed to 'observedTimestamp', so how does it get picked?
do we need this file in the project?
We are not validating anything in this test case. Any other way to test it?
Is it possible to use string interpolation here?
In the OOM, we should provide the complete xml with variables which will be populated by helm values.yaml. I am not sure how variables in this file will be used.
We can use @ContextConfiguration + @DynamicPropertySource to configure Kafka from test/application.yaml. It seems to work in the producer sure.
timestamp here is just the parameter name for the method to map a string to an offset date time. Any parameter name can be used here.
As discussed, this file is not used by defaut for dev deployments and is there for convenient reason to have a reference for secured configuration that is a requirement for Kafka connectivity.

I did not have a chance yet to figure out if this can have a performance impact.
Then, if is turned off by default for now.
As discussed this is changed for plaintext comfiguration by default.

If we are providing the complete xml in OOM, then the OOM file should not contain any variable and will then override this one.

For deployments that does not want to provide the complete xml, providing these environment variables or not providing them will also work.

From what I can see all options are available for deployment. Am I missing something else ?

The options that we have for logging are

1. Keep the current file and provide env variables in OOM & no logback.xml in OOM -> No option to fine tune logging based on the package for debugging without changing the OOM as logback.xml is not in configmap. 
If more detailed logging is required, changes in the deployment are required too to attach the logback.xml to the container.  

2. providing logback.xml in OOM -> User still needs to change the xml if more default logging ,based on package, is to be added but no change in the deployment. 

I prefer the option 2 because it is more flexible and most of the ONAP components are using it.

What I actually meant was 

"""{"${aDataName}":"${aDataValue}"}""" or 
"{\"${aDataName}\":\"${aDataValue}\"}"

It is a small json string so it does not make much difference.
I'm good with second option to give more flexibility. I'll remove the env vars then.
keep properties in alphabetical order please

Done.

When there is only one type of instance without any ambiguity, I often prefer a shorter and still meaningful name (also suggested by the IDE) and keep the code concise.

Several errors can be detected. All of them are collected in the unique InvalidEventEnvelopException instantiated in this method. Then, if needed, this unique exception is thrown. It has a reference to all errors collected (in invalidFields list property).
As per the discussion with Renu, environment variables has been removed from logback configuration.

In the other test (processing a valid event) we need to wait for the condition to become true after a certain period of time. If the condition is not finally satisfied this is an error.

Here, in this test, it is not the same scenario. We need to ensure that the condition remains true forever. As we can not wait forever, we arbitrary decided that waiting 3 sec would be enough to be confident that the condition is remaining true as expected. The verification being different, this is the reason why polling condition can not be used here.

Wait time is increased to 3 seconds after observing that the test to proceed a valid event is taking 1.5 sec in average to be completed.
This is done in EventFixures class that is used as a utility class. This approach had my preference over inheritance that is a much stronger and coupled design solution.

A precision is added to the specification.

Persistent layer is not able to create new data and this is highlighted by a null creation timestamp in network data entity returned. Hence, the presence of the 3 lines below in this block.

This is minor and not directly related to this change but it was intended. It is usually a good practice to have a newline at the end of a file.

Sonar rule exist for this, even if not enforced in the quality profile we are using:
https://sonarcloud.io/organizations/onap/rules?open=java%3AS113&rule_key=java%3AS113

https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/18743/whats-the-point-in-adding-a-new-line-to-the-end-of-a-file
Should we also include details and a message output when this exception gets thrown, just to add more detail to this exception in the logs.
Mentioned in comment in Exception Class

See comment on exception class.

Current message thrown is more of a 'cause'. Message could be 'Failed to create network data' with cause being what is currently implemented.
Should we also add details and a cause to this type of exception? If more services and implementations are added in the future it could make it easier to debug if this exception is also thrown there.
yes it was FYI (For Your Information) only, maybe to check if you are still following the latest Bell template (assuming something like that exist)

if the names are really long and awkward maybe but in general let try to use the full name for consistency (if we dont different developers tend to use different short versions in different part of the code)
sicne these are now in a sperate method you will have to use the 'assert' keyword!
ok, good to know I actually don't like additional whitespace in general but if this is  a SonarQube recommendation I am happy to follow it :-)
would be better to have all cps dependencies together..
isnt it possible to exclude all the generated-classes ?
Is this really required as we are not testing the retrieved result

It really depends on how we want to organize all these version properties. Here they are organized by type of dependencies (appllication, tests, plugin).

IMO, these properties are not really necessary when they are used only once. I would prefer having these inline in this case to remove verbosity and keep the pom more straightful to read.

In fact, this configuration was invalid and is removed.

Mapper class is tested with `CpsDataUpdatedEventMapperSpec`. Even if we only define interfaces or abstract classes, we still need to test that the implemented functionality is the expected one.
I dont think there is a specific template for Bell, we used to have the default ONAP one.

You're right, initially this was done to be able to access container db content when implementing the feature. 

It seems that accessing container db content will still be useful when implementing additional features or investigate bugs. To avoid having to rewrite the queries each time, it is kept here for later usage.

If something unexpected is occurring and the query is failing or an empty result is retrieved, jdbc template is still throwing an exception that is going to make the test failing.
I kind of agree, if version properties are only used once in the dependencies definition I don't think a property is helpful...
why exactly did you external port, is the a (security related) convention or was there a conflict?
does this mean the only way to overwrite this variable is now by editing this docker compose or pass in this variable on the command line
I think this is fine right now. We could add cause in as a separate parameter at another time if other classes throw this exception, and might have a different reason for failing
This is not security related. It is to be avoid port conflicts between core and temporal docker compose and to be able to have both db exposed on the same host, core on 5432 and temporal on 5433.

The name of the database is not configurable. It is set in application.yaml: 
  url: jdbc:postgresql://${DB_HOST}:${DB_PORT}/cpstemporaldb

POSTGRES_DB environment variable is used by PostgreSQL docker image to configure the default database for the container. See https://hub.docker.com/_/postgres documentation.

The same is done for cps core whose db name is set to `cpsdb`.
docker compose is ONLY used for local deployment and CIST test run, no need to be configurable
When this test is executed, the logs were showing related to Kafka connection. Is there any way to not to listen to Kafka when testing?
